title: capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. occidentalis
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Shepherd's Purse, is a biennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its heart-shaped seed pods and small, white, four-petaled flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, and is known to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Shepherd's Purse include its ability to reduce bleeding, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant effects, making it a useful remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Shepherd's Purse is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
